summary: Drew DeVault's December 2019 status update covers several concurrent projects. He introduced himitsu, a Plan 9-inspired secure key-value store for passwords and keys. He reports progress on SourceHut's single sign-on system and the upcoming DNS registration service names.sr.ht. He received a PinePhone and began developing 'Sway Mobile' with touch-friendly components. He also notes progress on aerc 0.3.0, the Wayland book, and a new compositor called sedna.
key_points: Himitsu is a new secure credential store inspired by Plan 9's factotum, designed so programs can establish authenticated connections without ever handling secret information directly
SourceHut shipped single sign-on across all sr.ht services and is building names.sr.ht for DNS and domain registration
The PinePhone arrived and DeVault began building 'Sway Mobile' — a touch-friendly compositor interface with a launcher (casa), on-screen keyboard, and new bar/lock screen
aerc 0.3.0 was released with development now almost entirely community-driven, demonstrating the project's growing contributor base
money_quotes: Your email client could ask himitsu to connect to an IMAP server, log in with your authentication details, then hand the authenticated file descriptor to the mail reader.
Your mail reader could work out of the box with zero configuration.
Work on this project will be slow going, as I have to use extra care to make sure that it's secure and correct.
"Sway Mobile" is now starting to take shape.
Improvements to aerc have been almost entirely community driven, and I've only stepped in to write a few small patches here and there.
I could never be this productive without your help.
key_insight: DeVault's projects consistently aim to give users direct control over their computing — from credential management that keeps secrets out of application code, to mobile interfaces built on open compositor technology, to community-driven email clients.
